title: Accessible Content Models, Landmarks, Headings, Links, Media, and Language · Behavior Case: Link Purpose Location
summary: Templates emit multiple mains, heading levels follow visual size, skip links target missing IDs, repeated links have vague labels, code lacks context, and media alternatives are never reviewed. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the link purpose location increment in WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Require meaningful link text, current-location evidence, multiple ways to find pages, breadcrumb semantics, and non-color state.
Enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-link-purpose-location — Require meaningful link text, current-location evidence, multiple ways to find pages, breadcrumb semantics, and non-color state.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-accessibility-semantics-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-accessibility-semantics-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_accessibility_semantics_5(pages: int = 8, checked_pages: int = 8, structural_findings: int = 0, media_decisions_missing: int = 0, human_tasks_passed: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or checked_pages < 0:
        return False, checked_pages, "page-count"
    if checked_pages != pages:
        return False, checked_pages, "accessibility-coverage"
    if structural_findings > 0 or media_decisions_missing > 0:
        return False, structural_findings + media_decisions_missing, "accessibility-finding"
    if human_tasks_passed < 3:
        return False, human_tasks_passed, "human-evidence"
    assert checked_pages == pages
    return True, human_tasks_passed, "accessible-content-model"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

The Link Purpose Location record says WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect link purpose location evidence boundary for Link Purpose Location.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require meaningful link text, current-location evidence, multiple ways to find pages, breadcrumb semantics, and non-color state.
